prompt:
Build a GovernanceIQ Education Data & Content Governance API that centralizes institutional analytics, data-quality controls, and controlled publication of alumni-facing content (including poetry/tribute entries) in a single offline-deployable backend. The service exposes domain-scoped interface capabilities for Authentication and Sessions, Users and Roles, Data Catalog and Metadata, Data Quality Rules and Inspections, Issue Tickets and Remediation Backfill, Lineage Tracking, Content (Poetry) Entries and Versions, Reporting and Scheduled Runs, and Audit/Monitoring. Roles are tiered as Administrator, Operations, and User: Administrators manage system configuration, permission grants, encryption/backup settings, and audit export; Operations manages data onboarding, quality rules, inspections, ticket triage, and scheduled reporting; Users can query approved datasets, run permitted reports, and read published poetry entries. Data quality governance supports rule types for completeness, consistency, uniqueness, numeric range, and distribution drift; each rule has a severity (P0–P3), a threshold (e.g., completeness must be ≥ 98.0%, uniqueness violations ≤ 0.5%), and a scope bound to a dataset and field(s). Automated inspections can be triggered on-demand or scheduled (default nightly at 2:00 AM local time) and must produce a 0–100 quality score computed from weighted rule results (default weights: P0=50, P1=30, P2=15, P3=5), plus a pass/fail gate (fail if any P0 breaches). Failed checks can automatically open issue tickets with owner, due date (default 7 calendar days), and remediation actions; backfill runs must be recorded with input fingerprint, affected record counts, and post-fix reinspection results. Poetry entries support draft/published state transitions, diff comparison, and rollback; only one published version may exist per entry at a time, publish requires a non-empty change reason (minimum 10 characters), and rollback is allowed only to versions created within the last 30 days.

The backend uses Django REST Framework to provide authenticated JSON-based APIs and MySQL for durable persistence, indexes, and transactional integrity across governance, reporting, and version history data. Core tables include users (unique username, salted password hash), sessions/tokens (8-hour expiry, revocable), datasets and fields (stable internal IDs), metadata (owner, retention class, sensitivity level), lineage edges (upstream/downstream references with timestamps), quality rules and inspection runs (immutable results), issue tickets (state machine: open/in_progress/blocked/resolved/closed), poetry entries and versions (operator, timestamp, changed fields, reason), report definitions and report runs (filters, time windows, permission scope), export jobs (CSV/XLSX, max 250,000 rows per file, 30-day retention), and append-only audit logs. Security requirements include rate limiting (default 120 requests/minute/user and 30 requests/minute/IP), strict input validation to prevent SQL injection and stored XSS, and encryption of sensitive fields at rest (AES-256) with TLS required for any network transport. Reliability requirements include nightly encrypted backups to local attached storage, point-in-time restore within the last 14 days, permission-controlled export access, optimistic concurrency control on mutable records, and idempotency keys (24-hour window) for create/update operations that would otherwise duplicate tickets, backfills, or report runs. Monitoring is fully offline: store and expose internal metrics such as ingestion/inspection success rate, export success rate, and recommendation CTR computed from local event logs, with audit-log export available to Administrators only.
